summary: Add a GDPR data export endpoint so users can request all their data as JSON. Implementation is 'just a handler,' but the compliance scope is high: GDPR Art. 15 right-to-access, identity verification, consent/auth checks, audit logging, and rate-limiting to prevent abuse.

reversibility.why: Endpoint can be disabled; data already sent cannot be recalled, so any bug in the export shape is permanent for that user.

blast_radius.why: New endpoint on its own path; does not change existing auth flows, but exposes every table the user touches.

compliance_scope.why: GDPR data-subject access request is explicitly in regulatory scope — Art. 15 timing and completeness requirements apply.

user_facing_impact.why: Users directly invoke the endpoint and receive their data.

novelty.why: DSAR handlers have a known shape, but every app's data surface differs.

scope_effort.why: Handler + identity verification + audit logging + rate-limiting + docs.

operational_risk.why: Abuse potential (enumeration, DoS) requires rate-limiting and monitoring.

coordination_cost.why: Compliance + privacy + audit + security + engineering all touch the plan.

specialists.name: requirements-analyst
specialists.why: Capture DSAR requirements and REQ- IDs mapped to GDPR articles.

specialists.name: product-manager
specialists.why: Own user-facing narrative, rate-limit policy, and rollout.

specialists.name: solution-architect
specialists.why: Design data-assembly path and identity-verification flow.

specialists.name: backend-engineer
specialists.why: Implement handler, assembly, and audit logging.

specialists.name: security-engineer
specialists.why: Threat-model abuse scenarios; enforce rate-limit and identity checks.

specialists.name: compliance-officer
specialists.why: Attest GDPR Art. 15 coverage and produce traceability artifacts.

specialists.name: privacy-expert
specialists.why: Validate what belongs in the export and data-minimization boundaries.

specialists.name: auditor
specialists.why: Ensure audit trail is sufficient for later regulatory inspection.

specialists.name: test-strategist
specialists.why: Shape pyramid including security and acceptance.

specialists.name: technical-writer
specialists.why: User-facing docs for how to invoke the export.

phases.name: clarify
phases.why: Pin GDPR Art. 15 obligations, identity verification method, and retention policy.

phases.name: design
phases.why: Data-assembly design, identity-verification flow, rate-limit model, audit logging.

phases.name: test-strategy
phases.why: Plan pyramid including security scan and compliance traceability.

phases.name: build
phases.why: Implement handler, checks, audit trail, and docs.

phases.name: review
phases.why: Compliance + privacy + audit sign-off before enabling.

rigor_why: Named regulatory surface (GDPR) + user-visible + identity-verification + audit-logging — compliance scope HIGH forces full rigor regardless of other factors.
complexity_why: Mid-range effort with compliance amplifier; not as cross-cutting as the auth rewrite but squarely in the regulatory surface.
open_questions: Identity verification method — reuse existing session, force re-auth, or a time-boxed token?
Retention and re-request throttle — how often can a user request a new export?
yolo_refusal_reason: rigor_tier=full with named regulatory surface (GDPR). Auto-proceed is ignored for full-rigor work; user confirmation + compliance officer sign-off are mandatory.

tasks.title: Clarify GDPR Art. 15 scope, identity verification, retention
Design data-assembly, identity verification, rate-limit, audit logging
Implement GDPR export handler + audit logging + rate-limiting
Acceptance test: user requests, receives, and verifies export
Compliance traceability package (REQ → design → code → test for GDPR Art. 15)
